author: Dr. Robert Feldman
content:
ANNUAL PHYSICAL — TOWSON INTERNAL MEDICINE ASSOCIATES

SUBJECTIVE: 71-year-old woman for annual visit. Doing better with grief; back to driving herself everywhere, water aerobics, and church activities. Brings her home blood-pressure log: systolics frequently in the 150s-160s despite reporting good adherence. No chest pain, palpitations, syncope, or edema.

OBJECTIVE: BP 162/92 in clinic. HR 78, regular. BMI 30. Heart regular, no murmur; lungs clear; no edema. Monofilament intact.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Hypertension, poorly controlled — three-drug regimen; optimized hydrochlorothiazide today; reinforced low-sodium diet; recheck in 6-8 weeks.
#T2DM — continue metformin; A1c at next labs.
#Osteopenia — continue calcium/vitamin D.
#Hyperlipidemia — continue atorvastatin; LDL at goal.
#Health maintenance — influenza vaccine given; due for screening colonoscopy follow-up.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Robert Feldman, MD. 10/20/2026.
